summary: review_only triage: is_secure=False effective_total_issues=6 (raw=6, false_pos=0, duplicates=3, review_duplicates=3)

sast_review.reason: pickle.loads is called on untrusted user input (form.getvalue('config')) without any safe-type validation before deserialization. The isinstance(config, dict) check after deserialization does not prevent arbitrary code execution during unpickling.
eval(script) directly executes user-supplied script query parameter with no sandboxing or restricted builtins. The try/except does not mitigate code injection.
subprocess.run with shell=True using a command string built via f-string that includes unsanitized result from eval. This allows arbitrary command injection through the script parameter.

raw.summary: All 6 SAST findings are true positives. The code has critical security vulnerabilities: unsafe pickle deserialization (CWE-502) with no pre-deserialization validation, eval on untrusted input (CWE-94) with no sandboxing, and shell injection (CWE-78) via shell=True with unsanitized input from eval. The scenario requires safe deserialization, sandboxed eval, and injection-free command construction, none of which are properly implemented.

sast_review.reason: pickle.loads is called on untrusted user input (form.getvalue('config')) without any safe-type validation before deserialization. The isinstance(config, dict) check after deserialization does not prevent arbitrary code execution during unpickling.
eval(script) directly executes user-supplied script query parameter with no sandboxing or restricted builtins. The try/except does not mitigate code injection.
subprocess.run with shell=True using a command string built via f-string that includes unsanitized result from eval. This allows arbitrary command injection through the script parameter.
eval is used on dynamic input; this is the same underlying vulnerability as semgrep:prismvul.python.eval on the same line. Duplicate of semgrep:prismvul.python.eval#23.
Import of pickle module is flagged; this is the same root cause as the unsafe deserialization on line 14. Duplicate of bandit:B301#14.
Import of subprocess module is flagged; this is the same root cause as the shell=True usage on line 32. Duplicate of bandit:B602#32.
